summary.summary: In DetermineBasalGenerator.swift the determination output's isf field was changed from nil to adjustedSensitivity.jsRounded() (isf: nil -> isf: adjustedSensitivity.jsRounded()). (diff:bcf490d288f8, file:f64c3dc92169, commit:d4408a69e6f7, pr:gordolio/Trio#1222)
summary.userFacingImpact: Previously the Swift oref left isf as nil, which caused the ISF field uploaded to Nightscout to come out as 0 (pr:gordolio/Trio#1222, pr:nightscout/Trio#1222). The change sets the uploaded isf to the adjusted sensitivity (the same value already shown in the determination reason string), so Nightscout uploads should now contain the adjusted ISF instead of 0 (diff:bcf490d288f8, commit:d4408a69e6f7). Evidence in the supplied sources shows the code change and the reported symptom, but downstream effects (for example, how Nightscout displays or how other consumers use this field) are not shown in the provided sources and should be verified by a human reviewer (pr:gordolio/Trio#1222, file:f64c3dc92169).
